# Feature Specification: ML Top 10 Coverage Bundle (OWASP Machine Learning Security Top 10:2023)

**Feature Branch**: `232-ml-top-10-coverage-bundle`
**Created**: 2026-04-27
**Status**: Draft
**Input**: User description: "PRD: 232 - ml-top-10-coverage-bundle"
**PRD**: [docs/product/02_PRD/232-ml-top-10-coverage-bundle-2026-04-27.md](../../docs/product/02_PRD/232-ml-top-10-coverage-bundle-2026-04-27.md)
**BLP-01 Phase**: Tier 2 ‚ÄĒ first Tier 2 feature (follows Tier 1 closure at F-1 / F-2 / F-3 / F-4 / F-5); **third execution** of the Heuristic A enrichment branch (after F-3 single-agent and F-5 two-agent), **first at three-agent scope** ‚ÄĒ closes ML01 / ML03 / ML04 / ML07 / ML08 Planned ‚Üí Covered and ML06 Partial ‚Üí Covered, transitioning OWASP ML Top 10:2023 to 10/10 Covered milestone (combined with post-F-5 OWASP AI top-10 = 20/20: post-F-6 tachi covers **30 of 30 entries across all three top-10 frameworks**).

## User Scenarios & Testing *(mandatory)*

### User Story 1 ‚ÄĒ Adversarial-ML Threat Coverage on a Predictive-ML Architecture (Priority: P0)

A security analyst threat-models a predictive-ML application architecture (training pipeline + MLOps registry + prediction-API serving classifier + active-learning feedback loop). The analyst wants tachi to surface findings covering the **full OWASP ML Top 10:2023 surface** (ML01 input manipulation, ML03 model inversion, ML04 membership inference, ML06 supply chain ‚ÄĒ both corpus-side and artifact-side facets, ML07 transfer learning, ML08 model skewing) through three existing host agents (`tampering`, `data-poisoning`, `model-theft`) ‚ÄĒ without a new agent, without fragmenting findings across six dispatch surfaces, and without scope creep on the host agents' existing surfaces.

**Why this priority**: This is the primary reason F-6 exists. Without three-agent enrichment of the ML Top 10 surface, the BLP-01 Coverage Matrix six rows remain Planned/Partial, the OWASP ML Top 10:2023 framework rollup stalls at 4/10, and the strategic 30/30 OWASP three-framework milestone (combined with post-F-5 OWASP AI top-10 = 20/20) cannot land. P0 because the feature has no MVP without this story.

**Independent Test**: Given a predictive-ML architecture exhibiting one or more ML topology indicators (e.g., MLOps registry without signed-artifact policy, fine-tuning step on pretrained weights without checksum verification, prediction API without per-tenant rate limit, active-learning loop without feedback-data integrity gates), running `/tachi.threat-model` emits ‚Č•1 new `T-{N}` finding (Cat 10) AND ‚Č•1 new `D-{N}` finding (Cat 8/9/10) AND ‚Č•1 new `LLM-{N}` finding (Cat 12/13/14), each citing the appropriate `OWASP ML0X:2023` primary in its prose-level `references:` array. Given an architecture with **no predictive-ML topology** (no MLOps registry, no fine-tuning step, no feature store, no prediction-API serving a classifier/regressor, no active-learning loop), the new Pattern Categories emit **zero findings** ‚ÄĒ Cat 1‚Äď9 (tampering), Cat 1‚Äď7 (data-poisoning), and Cat 1‚Äď11 (model-theft) fire as they do today.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** an architecture with a fine-tuning step that loads pretrained weights from HuggingFace Hub without checksum verification, **when** the orchestrator dispatches `data-poisoning`, **then** at least one new `D-{N}` finding emits from Pattern Category 8 (Transfer Learning Supply Chain) with its prose-level `references:` array containing `OWASP ML07:2023` (catalog-resolvable) and **MITRE ATLAS AML.T0019** named in mitigation prose only (not in `references` because T0019 is not catalog-resolvable per `schemas/taxonomy/mitre-atlas.yaml`).
2. **Given** an architecture with an active-learning loop where production predictions feed back into training without integrity gates, **when** `data-poisoning` runs, **then** at least one new `D-{N}` finding emits from Pattern Category 9 (Feedback-Loop Model Skewing) citing `OWASP ML08:2023` with `MITRE ATLAS AML.T0020` in `references` (catalog-resolvable) and `MITRE ATLAS AML.T0031` in mitigation prose only (not catalog-resolvable).
3. **Given** an architecture with a prediction API serving a classifier with no per-tenant rate limit and no output-perturbation defense, **when** `model-theft` runs, **then** at least one new `LLM-{N}` finding emits from Pattern Category 12 (Model Inversion, citing `OWASP ML03:2023` + `MITRE ATLAS AML.T0024`) AND at least one new `LLM-{N}` finding emits from Pattern Category 13 (Membership Inference, citing `OWASP ML04:2023` + `MITRE ATLAS AML.T0024`); both findings share the same architectural footprint (prediction API endpoint) but the `mitigation` narrative on each makes the disjoint architectural-tells explicit (Cat 12 = input reconstruction; Cat 13 = training-set membership determination).
4. **Given** an architecture with an MLOps model registry promoting models without signed-artifact policy, **when** `model-theft` runs, **then** at least one new `LLM-{N}` finding emits from Pattern Category 14 (Predictive-ML Artifact Supply Chain) citing `OWASP ML06:2023` (artifact-side facet) + `MITRE ATT&CK T1195` with mitigation referencing model-signing (Sigstore-style or KMS-backed) and registry IAM with promotion-gate review.
5. **Given** an architecture with a prediction API ingesting raw user input directly into a deployed classifier with no input-validation barrier, **when** `tampering` runs, **then** at least one new `T-{N}` finding emits from Pattern Category 10 (Adversarial Input Manipulation, Predictive ML) citing `OWASP ML01:2023` with `MITRE ATLAS AML.T0015` named in mitigation prose only (not catalog-resolvable).
6. **Given** the BLP-01 Coverage Matrix at `_internal/strategy/BLP-01-threat-coverage.md` ¬ß6, **when** F-6 is delivered, **then** rows for ML01, ML03, ML04, ML07, ML08 transition from **Planned ‚Üí Covered** and ML06 transitions from **Partial ‚Üí Covered**, with F-6 (Feature 232) named as the closure feature for all six.

---

### User Story 2 ‚ÄĒ Three-Agent Enrichment Without New Agents, Schema Bumps, or Orchestrator Changes (Priority: P0)

A tachi maintainer reviewing the F-6 PR diff wants to see exactly **six material file edits** across three host agents and three companion files plus one new ADR ‚ÄĒ and **zero edits** to schemas, orchestrator dispatch, consumers list, or any of the 22 non-target detection-tier files. The maintainer also wants explicit Pattern Category Disambiguation subsections on each of the three companion files so the boundary between new ML categories and pre-existing categories is unambiguous.

**Why this priority**: Equal priority to Story 1 because the structural integrity of the Heuristic A enrichment-branch protocol ‚ÄĒ at three-agent fan-out (the upper-bound demonstrated scope before F-7's planned 5-agent fan-out) ‚ÄĒ gates whether the protocol has been validated for future Tier 2 / Tier 3 features. The 22-file zero-edit invariant and the no-schema-bump invariant are the concrete deliverables that prove the protocol scales linearly without inflating tier-cap pressure or introducing schema/orchestrator coupling. Equal P0 because the protocol-validation deliverable is itself the BLP-01 strategic outcome that F-6 ships, alongside the pattern-coverage outcome of Story 1.

**Independent Test**: Given the post-F-5 detection-tier inventory of 28 files, the F-6 PR diff shows exactly 6 files materially modified (3 host agents + 3 companions); the other 22 detection-tier files have zero byte changes (grep-checkable). `schemas/finding.yaml` `schema_version` remains `"1.8"` and `id.pattern` regex remains unchanged. Each of the three modified companion files contains a new "Pattern Category Disambiguation" subsection (mirrors F-3 ADR-032 D7 + F-5 ADR-034 D7 precedent) explicitly drawing the boundary between the new ML categories and the pre-existing categories on that companion. ADR-035 is committed with the canonical 8-row mapping table + Heuristic A three-agent narrative + ML06 two-facet disjoint-tells decision + ML03/ML04 disjoint-tells decision.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** the post-F-5 detection-tier inventory of 28 files, **when** I run `git diff main HEAD -- '.claude/agents/tachi/' '.claude/skills/tachi-*/references/'`, **then** exactly 6 files appear in the diff: `.claude/agents/tachi/tampering.md`, `.claude/agents/tachi/data-poisoning.md`, `.claude/agents/tachi/model-theft.md`, and the matching three `detection-patterns.md` companions. The other 22 detection-tier files have zero byte changes.
2. **Given** the line-cap rules in ADR-023 Decision 1, **when** I check the three modified agent files post-edit, **then** `tampering.md` ‚Č§ 120 lines (STRIDE tier; PRD baseline 51, expected 54-58), `data-poisoning.md` ‚Č§ 150 lines (AI tier; baseline 78, expected 84-90), `model-theft.md` ‚Č§ 150 lines (AI tier; baseline 97, expected 103-108).
3. **Given** the schema invariant for finding IDs, **when** I check `schemas/finding.yaml`, **then** `schema_version` remains `"1.8"` and `id.pattern` regex remains `^(S|T|R|I|D|E|AG|LLM|AGP|OI|MI|TE)-\d+$` ‚ÄĒ no new ID prefix added (F-6 is the third no-schema-bump enrichment after F-3 and F-5; the first at three-agent scope).
4. **Given** the orchestrator-tier files (`.claude/agents/tachi/orchestrator.md`, `.claude/skills/tachi-orchestration/references/dispatch-rules.md`, `.claude/skills/tachi-shared/references/finding-format-shared.md`), **when** I check the F-6 PR diff, **then** zero functional changes appear; all three host agents are already dispatch-registered and consumer-listed.
5. **Given** each of the three modified companion files post-edit, **when** I look for a "Pattern Category Disambiguation" subsection, **then** each file contains one: `tampering` companion disambiguates Cat 10 from Cat 1-9 (Adversarial Input Manipulation against predictive ML vs. generic injection / deserialization / supply-chain); `data-poisoning` companion disambiguates Cat 8/9/10 from Cat 1-7 (predictive-ML training poisoning + transfer-learning + feedback-loop + corpus-supply-chain vs. existing LLM/RAG poisoning + backdoor triggers); `model-theft` companion disambiguates Cat 12/13/14 from Cat 1-11 (predictive-ML extraction + artifact supply-chain vs. existing LLM extraction + cost-amplification).
6. **Given** ADR-035 lands as Proposed before pattern-catalog edits and transitions to Accepted at PR merge, **when** the ADR's Decision section is read, **then** it contains: (a) the canonical 8-row sub-pattern ‚Üí owning-agent mapping table with reference rows for ML02/05/09/10 closures, (b) explicit ML06 two-facet disjoint architectural-tells decision (corpus-side Cat 10 owns dataset-repos / feature-stores / training-data path; artifact-side Cat 14 owns model-registry / weight-artifact-storage / serving-time integrity), (c) explicit ML03 vs ML04 disjoint architectural-tells decision (Cat 12 = white-box gradient inversion + black-box optimization for input reconstruction; Cat 13 = confidence-thresholding + shadow-model attacks for training-set membership determination), (d) the Heuristic A three-agent narrative with cross-references to ADR-023 D3, ADR-030 D1, ADR-032, ADR-034 (with explicit citation of ADR-034 lines 192‚Äď204 forecast).

---

### User Story 3 ‚ÄĒ Byte-Identical Regeneration on Non-Predictive-ML Baselines + New `predictive-ml-app/` Mutation Target (Priority: P0)

A tachi adopter running the existing pipeline on an architecture without a predictive-ML topology wants byte-identical output before and after F-6 ships (no spurious new findings, no PDF page-count changes, no SARIF diffs). The same adopter runs tachi on the new `examples/predictive-ml-app/` architecture (authored at plan day per Q1 RESOLVED) and sees ‚Č•6 new ML findings (‚Č•1 per host agent across the 6 closed ML Top 10 items), demonstrating downstream-user end-to-end coverage of the OWASP ML Top 10:2023 framework.

**Why this priority**: Equal priority to Stories 1 and 2 because byte-identity preservation gates adopter trust in the enrichment-branch protocol ‚ÄĒ every prior enrichment (F-3 / F-5) shipped under the same invariant, and breaking it would invalidate the additive-only edit discipline foundation (ADR-023 D3). The `predictive-ml-app/` mutation target gates the SC-019 demonstration deliverable (‚Č•6 new ML findings on a real architecture, not a synthetic test fixture) ‚ÄĒ which is the structural validation that the new Pattern Categories actually emit findings against the indicators they declare. Equal P0 because adopters running tachi on non-ML architectures cannot tolerate spurious findings, and adopters running tachi on predictive-ML architectures cannot tolerate missing coverage.

**Independent Test**: The 6 example baselines (`web-app`, `microservices`, `ascii-web-api`, `mermaid-agentic-app`, `free-text-microservice`, `maestro-reference`) regenerate **byte-identically** under `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1700000000` per ADR-021 ‚ÄĒ verified via `tests/scripts/test_backward_compatibility.py`. The new `examples/predictive-ml-app/` architecture (authored at plan day Tuesday 2026-04-28 by architect + senior-backend-engineer co-authoring) regenerates with ‚Č•6 new ML findings: ‚Č•1 new `T-{N}` (Cat 10), ‚Č•1 new `D-{N}` (Cat 8 OR 9 OR 10), and ‚Č•1 new `LLM-{N}` (Cat 12 OR 13 OR 14), with aggregate target ‚Č•6 across the six closed ML Top 10 items. The `predictive-ml-app/` baseline is excluded from the byte-identity loop in `test_backward_compatibility.py` (mirrors the `agentic-app` and `consumer-agent-app` mutation-target precedents).

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** the 6 example baselines, **when** I run `tests/scripts/test_backward_compatibility.py` under `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1700000000` per ADR-021, **then** the test passes 6/6 byte-identical against `security-report.pdf.baseline` files for all 6 architectures.
2. **Given** the predictive-ML emission gate (Categories 10 / 8-9-10 / 12-13-14 fire only when predictive-ML topology indicators are present), **when** I check threats.md output for the 6 non-ML baselines, **then** no new `T-{N}` Cat-10, `D-{N}` Cat-8/9/10, or `LLM-{N}` Cat-12/13/14 findings appear.
3. **Given** the new `examples/predictive-ml-app/architecture.md` (Q1 RESOLVED at PRD time per architect v1 HIGH-1 finding: `agentic-app` has zero predictive-ML signal verified via empirical grep on 13 indicators), **when** I regenerate `predictive-ml-app/` end-to-end, **then** at least 6 new ML-category findings emit (‚Č•1 per host agent: tampering Cat 10, data-poisoning Cat 8/9/10, model-theft Cat 12/13/14), and the regen produces a `security-report.pdf.baseline` file that is intentionally **excluded** from `test_backward_compatibility.py`'s byte-identity loop (mirrors `agentic-app` + `consumer-agent-app` precedent).
4. **Given** the structural changes scope (Python scripts, Typst templates, schema), **when** I review the F-6 PR diff, **then** zero changes appear in `scripts/`, `templates/`, `schemas/finding.yaml`, `schemas/taxonomy/owasp.yaml` (existing ML01‚ÄďML10 entries already present per F-A1 inventory), or any orchestrator dispatch table.
5. **Given** `examples/agentic-app/` (the F-3 / F-5 mutation target ‚ÄĒ F-6 does NOT touch agentic-app per architect MEDIUM-2 correction), **when** I check the F-6 PR diff, **then** zero changes appear under `examples/agentic-app/` (the agentic-app baseline is regenerated only as F-3 / F-5 / future mutation targets; F-6's mutation target is the new `predictive-ml-app/` only).
6. **Given** the new `examples/predictive-ml-app/architecture.md` exhibits all five predictive-ML topology indicators (training pipeline ingesting from dataset repo + fine-tuning step on pretrained weights + MLOps model registry + prediction-API endpoint serving classifier with no input-validation barrier + active-learning feedback loop), **when** all three host agents run, **then** the aggregate finding emission covers all 6 closed ML Top 10 items: ML01 via tampering Cat 10, ML07 via data-poisoning Cat 8, ML08 via data-poisoning Cat 9, ML06 (corpus) via data-poisoning Cat 10, ML03 via model-theft Cat 12, ML04 via model-theft Cat 13, ML06 (artifact) via model-theft Cat 14 (the 6 closed items map to 7 categories with ML06's two-facet split).

---

### Edge Cases

- **Architecture with both ML06 corpus-side AND ML06 artifact-side signals**: Both Cat 10 (data-poisoning) and Cat 14 (model-theft) fire. They are not duplicates ‚ÄĒ the disjoint architectural-tells per ADR-035 D-numbered decision dictate: corpus-side Cat 10 owns dataset-repos / feature-stores / training-data path; artifact-side Cat 14 owns model-registry / weight-artifact-storage / serving-time integrity. The same architecture may legitimately surface both findings describing distinct supply-chain layers.
- **Architecture with both ML03 (model inversion) AND ML04 (membership inference) signals on the same prediction API**: Both Cat 12 (model-theft) and Cat 13 (model-theft) fire. They are not duplicates ‚ÄĒ Cat 12's architectural-tells (white-box gradient inversion + black-box optimization for input reconstruction) and Cat 13's architectural-tells (confidence-thresholding + shadow-model attacks for training-set membership determination) are disjoint per ADR-035 D-numbered decision. Shared `MITRE ATLAS AML.T0024` citation is appropriate (both exfiltrate via inference API) but the architectural-tells must be distinguishable to prevent duplicate findings on the same prediction-API endpoint.
- **Pattern Category Disambiguation ‚ÄĒ tampering Cat 10 vs Cat 1-9**: Cat 10 (Adversarial Input Manipulation, Predictive ML) fires on **predictive-ML inference-time evasion** (small-perturbation adversarial examples, FGSM/PGD-style attacks, decision-boundary attacks against deployed classifiers) where the architectural-tell is a deployed predictive ML classifier + inference endpoint + no input-validation barrier. Cat 9 (Injection Attacks: XSS, SQLi, Command Injection ‚ÄĒ pre-existing) fires on **generic web-application injection** with the architectural-tell of a generic API endpoint without any predictive-ML signal. The same architecture may legitimately surface both Cat 9 + Cat 10 findings if it has both a generic API surface and a predictive-ML inference endpoint.
- **Pattern Category Disambiguation ‚ÄĒ data-poisoning Cat 8/9/10 vs Cat 1-7**: Cat 8 (Transfer Learning Supply Chain) fires on **predictive-ML pretrained-weights** ingestion (HuggingFace Hub / TensorFlow Hub) without checksum verification ‚ÄĒ distinct from Cat 6 (RAG corpus poisoning) which fires on LLM retrieval-augmented-generation knowledge stores. Cat 9 (Feedback-Loop Model Skewing) fires on **predictive-ML active-learning loops** ‚ÄĒ distinct from Cat 7 (Backdoor Triggers via Prompt Injection) which fires on LLM-tier backdoor triggers. Cat 10 (Predictive-ML Supply Chain Completeness) fires on **dataset repositories / feature stores / MLOps registry corpus-side** ‚ÄĒ distinct from Cat 6 (LLM RAG corpus poisoning).
- **Pattern Category Disambiguation ‚ÄĒ model-theft Cat 12/13/14 vs Cat 1-11**: Cat 12 (Model Inversion) fires on **predictive-ML training-data input reconstruction** ‚ÄĒ distinct from Cat 1 (LLM weight-leak via debugging interfaces) and Cat 2-9 (LLM extraction attacks). Cat 13 (Membership Inference) fires on **predictive-ML training-set membership determination** ‚ÄĒ distinct from any LLM-extraction category. Cat 14 (Predictive-ML Artifact Supply Chain) fires on **predictive-ML model-registry / weight-artifact integrity** ‚ÄĒ distinct from Cat 10 (Cost Amplification) and Cat 11 (Denial-of-Wallet) which are LLM-tier cost-DoW attacks from F-5.
- **F-A2 referential validation on absent ATLAS catalog IDs**: every Cat 10 (T) + Cat 8/9/10 (D) + Cat 12/13/14 (LLM) finding's `references` array MUST cite only IDs verified present in `schemas/taxonomy/{owasp,mitre-atlas,mitre-attack}.yaml`. PRD-time + plan-time verification: ML01-ML10 are catalog-resolvable; AML.T0018, T0020, T0024 are catalog-resolvable; **AML.T0015, T0019, T0031 are NOT catalog-resolvable** (3 of 6 ATLAS techniques) and are named in `mitigation` narratives as text-only cross-references (mirrors F-5's T1496 prose-only handling at 3x scale).
- **6 non-predictive-ML baselines preserve byte-identity**: `web-app`, `microservices`, `ascii-web-api`, `mermaid-agentic-app`, `free-text-microservice`, `maestro-reference` do not exhibit predictive-ML topologies (no MLOps registry, no fine-tuning pipeline, no feature store, no prediction-API serving a classifier/regressor, no active-learning loop). New Cat 10 (T) + Cat 8/9/10 (D) + Cat 12/13/14 (LLM) emit zero findings on all 6 baselines. All 6 PDFs regenerate byte-identically under `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1700000000` per ADR-021.
- **`agentic-app` zero-touch on F-6**: F-3 mutation target + F-5 mutation target. Architect MEDIUM-2 correction: agentic-app baseline exists at `examples/agentic-app/sample-report/security-report.pdf.baseline` but is excluded from `test_backward_compatibility.py`'s byte-identity loop because it is the F-3 / F-5 mutation target. **F-6 does not touch agentic-app** ‚ÄĒ the new `predictive-ml-app/` is the F-6 mutation target.
- **Existing categories byte-identity preservation (ADR-023 D3)**: `tampering` Cat 1-9 + Overview + Targeted DFD Element Types + Trigger Keywords + Primary Sources sections remain byte-identical pre/post edit; `data-poisoning` Cat 1-7 + same structural sections byte-identical; `model-theft` Cat 1-11 (post-F-5) + same structural sections byte-identical. Grep-checkable invariant via structural diff returning empty for unchanged regions.
- **R5 contingency pre-named deferral pair**: If R5 (Heuristic A three-agent emergent issues materialize during build) triggers, the deferral candidates are **`data-poisoning` Cat 10 + `model-theft` Cat 14** ‚ÄĒ both ML06 facets, deferred together to preserve the ML06 two-facet split coherence (R4) by shipping it together in a follow-on PR rather than fragmenting it. Ship 5 of 7 categories closing ML01 + ML07 + ML08 + ML03 + ML04; ML06 closure deferred to follow-on. Pre-naming removes adjudication overhead from the risk-trigger moment.
- **Line count caps**: `tampering.md` post-edit MUST remain ‚Č§120 lines (STRIDE tier cap per ADR-023; PRD baseline 51, expected 54-58). `data-poisoning.md` post-edit MUST remain ‚Č§150 lines (AI tier cap; baseline 78, expected 84-90). `model-theft.md` post-edit MUST remain ‚Č§150 lines (AI tier cap; baseline 97, expected 103-108). Tightest margin is `model-theft.md` at ‚Č•42 lines ‚ÄĒ comfortable.

## Requirements *(mandatory)*

### Functional Requirements

- **FR-001**: The system MUST extend the metadata YAML in `.claude/agents/tachi/tampering.md` with `OWASP ML01:2023 ‚ÄĒ Input Manipulation Attack` and `MITRE ATLAS AML.T0015 ‚ÄĒ Evade ML Model` appended to the `owasp_references` list. Pre-existing entries MUST be preserved byte-identically (grep-checkable). The agent file post-edit line count MUST remain ‚Č§120 (STRIDE tier cap per ADR-023). PRD-time baseline 51 lines; expected post-edit 54-58 lines.
- **FR-002**: The system MUST extend the `## Purpose` section in `.claude/agents/tachi/tampering.md` with a 1‚Äď3 line extension naming the adversarial input manipulation surface for predictive ML alongside the existing data-tampering surface. The pre-existing `## Purpose` prose paragraph MUST remain byte-identical (additive append, not rewrite).
- **FR-003**: The system MUST extend the Detection Workflow Step 5 references list in `.claude/agents/tachi/tampering.md` with `OWASP ML01:2023, MITRE ATLAS AML.T0015` exemplar mention. Existing references MUST be preserved byte-identically.
- **FR-004**: The system MUST append **Pattern Category 10 ‚ÄĒ Adversarial Input Manipulation (Predictive ML)** to `.claude/skills/tachi-tampering/references/detection-patterns.md` after existing Pattern Category 9. Existing Pattern Categories 1‚Äď9 plus the file's `## Overview`, `## Targeted DFD Element Types`, `## Trigger Keywords`, and `## Primary Sources` sections MUST remain byte-identical pre/post edit per ADR-023 Decision 3 (grep-checkable additive-only invariant). Pattern Category 10 MUST carry: (a) ‚Č•4 indicators including deployed predictive ML classifier + inference endpoint + no input-validation barrier + missing adversarial-defense controls; (b) ‚Č•1 worked example demonstrating an adversarial input manipulation surface against a deployed classifier; (c) primary-source citation `OWASP ML01:2023 ‚ÄĒ Input Manipulation Attack`; (d) related citation **MITRE ATLAS AML.T0015 ‚ÄĒ Evade ML Model** named in mitigation prose only (NOT in `references` because T0015 is not catalog-resolvable per `schemas/taxonomy/mitre-atlas.yaml`); (e) named adversarial-defense mitigations distinct from generic tampering controls (adversarial training, statistical anomaly detection on inputs, distribution-shift monitoring, confidence-thresholding with HITL escalation, ensemble disagreement detection).
- **FR-005**: The system MUST extend the metadata YAML in `.claude/agents/tachi/data-poisoning.md` with `OWASP ML06:2023 ‚ÄĒ AI Supply Chain Attacks`, `OWASP ML07:2023 ‚ÄĒ Transfer Learning Attack`, `OWASP ML08:2023 ‚ÄĒ Model Skewing`, `MITRE ATLAS AML.T0018 ‚ÄĒ Backdoor ML Model`, `MITRE ATLAS AML.T0019 ‚ÄĒ Publish Poisoned Datasets`, `MITRE ATLAS AML.T0020 ‚ÄĒ Poison Training Data`, `MITRE ATLAS AML.T0031 ‚ÄĒ Erode ML Model Integrity` appended to the `owasp_references` list. Pre-existing entries MUST be preserved byte-identically. The agent file post-edit line count MUST remain ‚Č§150 (AI tier cap per ADR-023). Baseline 78 lines; expected post-edit 84-90 lines.
- **FR-006**: The system MUST extend the `## Purpose` section in `.claude/agents/tachi/data-poisoning.md` with a 1‚Äď3 line extension naming the predictive-ML training poisoning, transfer-learning supply-chain, and feedback-loop skewing surfaces alongside the existing LLM/RAG poisoning surface. The pre-existing `## Purpose` prose paragraph MUST remain byte-identical. The system MUST extend Detection Workflow Step 5 references list with the new OWASP ML and MITRE ATLAS citations; existing references preserved byte-identically.
- **FR-007**: The system MUST append three new Pattern Categories to `.claude/skills/tachi-data-poisoning/references/detection-patterns.md` after existing Pattern Category 7: **Pattern Category 8 ‚ÄĒ Transfer Learning Supply Chain (Predictive ML)**, **Pattern Category 9 ‚ÄĒ Feedback-Loop Model Skewing (Active Learning / Online Learning)**, **Pattern Category 10 ‚ÄĒ Predictive-ML Supply Chain Completeness (Datasets, Feature Stores, MLOps Registry)**. Existing Pattern Categories 1‚Äď7 plus the file's structural sections MUST remain byte-identical pre/post edit per ADR-023 Decision 3. - **`data-poisoning` agent** (`.claude/agents/tachi/data-poisoning.md`) ‚ÄĒ existing AI-tier threat detection agent (78 lines pre-edit, ‚Č§150-line cap). F-6 enriches metadata `owasp_references` (7-line append: ML06/07/08 + ATLAS T0018/T0019/T0020/T0031), `## Purpose` (1‚Äď3 line extension), and Detection Workflow Step 5 references. Outputs `D-{N}` findings.
- **`model-theft` agent** (`.claude/agents/tachi/model-theft.md`) ‚ÄĒ existing AI-tier threat detection agent (97 lines pre-edit post-F-5, ‚Č§150-line cap). F-6 enriches metadata `owasp_references` (4-line append: ML03/04/06 + ATLAS T0024), `## Purpose` (1‚Äď3 line extension), and Detection Workflow Step 5 references. Outputs `LLM-{N}` findings.
- **`tachi-tampering` skill** ‚ÄĒ existing companion (190 lines pre-edit). F-6 appends Cat 10 + Pattern Category Disambiguation subsection + Primary Sources entry.
- **`tachi-data-poisoning` skill** ‚ÄĒ existing companion (137 lines pre-edit). F-6 appends Cat 8 + Cat 9 + Cat 10 + Pattern Category Disambiguation subsection + 3 Primary Sources entries.
- **`tachi-model-theft` skill** ‚ÄĒ existing companion (211 lines pre-edit post-F-5). F-6 appends Cat 12 + Cat 13 + Cat 14 + Pattern Category Disambiguation subsection + 3 Primary Sources entries.
- **`T-{N}` finding** ‚ÄĒ existing finding-ID prefix for tampering findings. Single-namespace ID space; F-6 appends Cat 10. Sequential numbering across Cat 1-10.
- **`D-{N}` finding** ‚ÄĒ existing finding-ID prefix for data-poisoning findings. Single-namespace ID space; F-6 appends Cat 8 + Cat 9 + Cat 10. Sequential numbering across Cat 1-10.
- **`LLM-{N}` finding** ‚ÄĒ existing finding-ID prefix for model-theft findings (same prefix shared across LLM-tier agents; namespace partitioned by `category` field). Single-namespace ID space within model-theft; F-6 appends Cat 12 + Cat 13 + Cat 14. Sequential numbering across Cat 1-14.
- **Pattern Category 10 (tampering) ‚ÄĒ Adversarial Input Manipulation (Predictive ML)** ‚ÄĒ new tampering category. Indicators ‚Č•4. Primary: OWASP ML01:2023. Related: AML.T0015 (prose-only).
- **Pattern Categories 8/9/10 (data-poisoning) ‚ÄĒ Transfer Learning Supply Chain / Feedback-Loop Model Skewing / Predictive-ML Supply Chain Completeness** ‚ÄĒ three new data-poisoning categories. Indicators ‚Č•4 each. Primaries: ML07 / ML08 / ML06 corpus-side. Related: T0018+T0019 (Cat 8) / T0020+T0031 (Cat 9) / T1195 (Cat 10). T0019 + T0031 are prose-only.
- **Pattern Categories 12/13/14 (model-theft) ‚ÄĒ Model Inversion / Membership Inference / Predictive-ML Artifact Supply Chain** ‚ÄĒ three new model-theft categories. Indicators ‚Č•4 each. Primaries: ML03 / ML04 / ML06 artifact-side. Related: AML.T0024 (Cat 12 + Cat 13, shared but disjoint architectural-tells) / T1195 (Cat 14).
- **ADR-035** (`docs/architecture/02_ADRs/ADR-035-ml-top-10-coverage-bundle.md`) ‚ÄĒ public per-feature ADR documenting the Heuristic A enrichment pattern at three-agent scope, the canonical 8-row sub-pattern ‚Üí owning-agent mapping table, the ML06 two-facet disjoint-tells decision, and the ML03/ML04 disjoint-tells decision. Authored under dual-commit Proposed ‚Üí Accepted protocol. Public-only; SDR-001 cross-reference lives in private companion docs per Option C governance.
- **Predictive-ML topology gate** ‚ÄĒ detection workflow invariant requiring predictive-ML indicators (training pipeline, MLOps registry, feature store, fine-tuning step on pretrained weights, active-learning loop, prediction-API endpoint serving classifier/regressor) before Cat 10 (T) + Cat 8/9/10 (D) + Cat 12/13/14 (LLM) emission. Non-predictive-ML architectures emit zero new findings.
- **Pattern Category Disambiguation subsections** (3 instances, one per host companion) ‚ÄĒ explicit non-overlap contracts mirroring F-3 D7 + F-5 D7 precedent. Documented inline in each companion file and recorded as ADR-035 D-numbered decision.
- **ML06 two-facet split** ‚ÄĒ bifurcation of OWASP ML06:2023 into corpus-side (data-poisoning Cat 10) and artifact-side (model-theft Cat 14) with disjoint architectural-tells per ADR-035 D-numbered decision. Same architecture may surface both findings without duplication.
- **`examples/predictive-ml-app/`** ‚ÄĒ new F-6 mutation-target example (Q1 RESOLVED). Architecture exhibits all 5 predictive-ML topology indicators. Authored at plan day; baseline excluded from `test_backward_compatibility.py` byte-identity loop (mirrors agentic-app + consumer-agent-app precedent).

## Out of Scope (Explicit)

The following items are explicitly out of scope for F-6. Each carries a forward-reference per PRD scope boundaries:

1. **Three new agents (`adversarial-input-manipulation`, `predictive-ml-poisoning`, `predictive-ml-extraction`)** ‚Üí explicitly REJECTED per Heuristic A consolidation; SDR-001 Decision 4 locked the resolution. Reopening this would re-adjudicate every prior Heuristic A consolidation (F-1 / F-2 / F-3 / F-4 / F-5).
2. **Schema bump 1.8 ‚Üí 1.9** ‚Üí F-6 reuses existing `T-{N}`, `D-{N}`, `LLM-{N}` prefixes; no new ID prefix introduced. F-6 is the third BLP-01 detection feature without a schema bump (after F-3 + F-5) and the first at three-agent enrichment scope.
3. **`finding-format-shared.md` consumers list edit** ‚Üí all three host agents already present (PRD-time + plan-time verified). No edit required.
4. **Functional orchestrator/dispatch-rules edits** ‚Üí all three agents already fully registered. Zero functional dispatch edits.
5. **`source_attribution` populator wiring on host agents** ‚Üí deferred to F-A3 per `schemas/finding.yaml` lines 230‚Äď238 and ADR-028 Decision 6. F-6 cites ML Top 10 references in prose-level `references:` array only. F-A3 dependency is one-way (F-6 ‚Üí F-A3 inheritance).
6. **MITRE ATLAS catalog augmentation for AML.T0015, AML.T0019, AML.T0031** ‚Üí out-of-scope for F-6; the 3 missing entries appear in mitigation prose only at 3x F-5's T1496 precedent scale. Future F-A1.1 follow-on may upgrade these to structured `references` entries.
7. **OWASP Mobile Top 10 bundle (F-7)** ‚Üí Tier 2 follow-on at planned 5-agent fan-out, deferred per BLP-01 ¬ßF-7. The Heuristic A enrichment-branch at three-agent scope precedent F-6 establishes informs but does not block F-7.
8. **Tier 3 features (F-8 through F-11)** ‚Üí Tier 3 cloud-security and supply-chain refinements, sequenced after F-7.
9. **Cross-feature attack-chain reasoning** ‚Üí Feature 141 cross-layer attack chains already covers this; new Cat 10 (T) + Cat 8/9/10 (D) + Cat 12/13/14 (LLM) findings flow through Phase 3.5 chain correlation automatically with zero F-6 scope.
10. **Catalog population for additional MITRE ATT&CK or CWE IDs** ‚Üí F-6 cites only IDs verified present at PRD time + plan time (ML01-ML10, AML.T0018/T0020/T0024, T1195 + sub-techniques). T0015/T0019/T0031 are named in mitigation prose only.
11. **Commercial framing in ADR-035** ‚Üí Layer 2, tachi Cloud, enterprise-only features explicitly excluded per public-ADR governance (Option C contract from SDR-001). Public ADR stands on technical merits only.
12. **agentic-app regeneration** ‚Üí architect MEDIUM-2 correction: agentic-app is the F-3/F-5 mutation target; F-6 does NOT touch agentic-app. The new `predictive-ml-app/` is the F-6 mutation target.
13. **`examples/consumer-agent-app/` regeneration** ‚Üí F-4's mutation target; untouched by F-6. Already excluded from `test_backward_compatibility.py` byte-identity loop.
14. **Re-enrichment of ML02 (Data Poisoning), ML05 (Model Theft), ML09 (Output Integrity), ML10 (Model Poisoning)** ‚Üí already Covered by existing pre-F-6 work (ML09 by F-1 `output-integrity`; ML02/05/10 by pre-BLP-01 work). F-6 ADR-035 mapping table includes reference rows for completeness but no re-enrichment authoring.
15. **R5 contingency deferral pair** (pre-named per team-lead MEDIUM-1): If R5 (Heuristic A three-agent emergent issues) triggers, defer **`data-poisoning` Cat 10 + `model-theft` Cat 14** (both ML06 facets) together to follow-on PR, preserving ML06 two-facet split coherence. Ship 5 of 7 categories closing ML01 + ML07 + ML08 + ML03 + ML04. Pre-naming removes adjudication overhead from risk-trigger moment.

## Assumptions

- The architect confirms Heuristic A enrichment-vs-new-agent distinctness is intact at three-agent scope (SDR-001 Decision 4 locked the resolution; ADR-030 + ADR-032 + ADR-034 reinforced the signal-class taxonomy; F-3 demonstrated single-agent, F-5 demonstrated two-agent, F-6 demonstrates three-agent) at the start of Day 1 build and commits ADR-035 Proposed at Day 1 AM. If a subsume-into-new-agent signal surfaces during pattern authoring, escalation to architect is required before Day 1 EOD (per PRD R5 Day 3 AM gate; R5 contingency pre-named deferral pair = data-poisoning Cat 10 + model-theft Cat 14).
- New `examples/predictive-ml-app/` is the regeneration target per Q1 RESOLVED at PRD time (architect HIGH-1 finding: agentic-app has zero predictive-ML signal). Architect + senior-backend-engineer co-author at plan day Tuesday 2026-04-28 (~4-6 hours effort; absorbed by 2026-05-04 buffer day if needed). The architecture exhibits all 5 predictive-ML topology indicators per FR-014. ADR-035 includes a Detection Calibration Note clarifying that the new Pattern Categories fire on **structural absence** of named control mechanisms ‚ÄĒ consistent with F-1 / F-2 / F-4 / F-5 absence-style detection style.
- Pattern Category indicator counts are ‚Č•4 per FR-004/007/010 (PRD-time architect-leaning: 4-5 indicators per category). Architect may add or refine indicators at plan time. Architect retains authority to merge or split categories at plan day if the indicator surface justifies ‚ÄĒ Q4 RESOLVED at PRD time as 7 categories total (1 + 3 + 3) but architect retains plan-day floor authority.
- Severity baseline: tampering Cat 10 default MEDIUM-HIGH (architectural-absence-driven); data-poisoning Cat 8/9/10 default HIGH (training-pipeline integrity is a high-impact surface); model-theft Cat 12/13 default MEDIUM-HIGH (data-extraction surface; severity escalates with sensitive training data); Cat 14 default HIGH (artifact supply-chain integrity at promotion gate). No CRITICAL floors on F-6 (asymmetric to F-5's Cat 11 2-condition CRITICAL floor for multi-tenant freemium). Architect may refine severity defaults at plan day.
- Pattern Category Disambiguation subsections (FR-011) are appended to each of the three companion files ‚ÄĒ three instances total. Architect adjudicates exact wording at plan day; default scope per FR-011 boundary descriptions in this spec.
- ADR-035 lands as Proposed at Day 1 AM (build start) to lock Heuristic A enrichment at three-agent scope before pattern-catalog authoring; transitions to Accepted at PR merge with provisional merge-date and post-merge SHA fill (mirrors ADR-027/028/029/030/031/032/033/034 precedent). - All catalog citations are verified present at PRD time + plan time: ML01-ML10 (10 entries) in `schemas/taxonomy/owasp.yaml`; AML.T0018, AML.T0020, AML.T0024 in `schemas/taxonomy/mitre-atlas.yaml`; T1195 + T1195.001 + T1195.002 in `schemas/taxonomy/mitre-attack.yaml`. **AML.T0015, AML.T0019, AML.T0031 are verified ABSENT** in `schemas/taxonomy/mitre-atlas.yaml` (3 of 6 ATLAS techniques) ‚ÄĒ named in mitigation prose only at 3x F-5 T1496 precedent scale.
